qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the English Word or OpenDocument template for Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) applications?
qa_pairs.answer: The English Word or OpenDocument template for RPP applications can be found at this link: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1tGMpMRnaLsso6U80vZiXbY7o1m9kVvvnEuXOUoUIKBo/edit?tab=t.0.

qa_pairs.question: What is the link for the French Word or OpenDocument template for Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) applications?
qa_pairs.answer: The French Word or OpenDocument template for RPP applications is available at: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1vueNU31dsMhL3nbyUYzF7NAzYbIbhl52tPrbDZWGhJI/edit?usp=drive_link.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I download the English LaTeX template for Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) applications?
qa_pairs.answer: The English LaTeX template for RPP applications is available here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/18idQJtVZmASgGW-7hVl3uwQdoQ7XTuvO/view?usp=drive_link.

qa_pairs.question: What is the link for the French LaTeX template for Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) applications?
qa_pairs.answer: The French LaTeX template for RPP applications can be downloaded from: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1TCzOu12LccRT0HuvpPK8gycv0kTAlRrc/view?usp=drive_link.

qa_pairs.question: How do I download an editable version of a Word or OpenDocument template from a Google document?
qa_pairs.answer: To download an editable version, open the non-editable Google document, then go to 'File → Download' in the main menu and select 'Microsoft Word' or 'OpenDocument Format'. Save it and start working in the downloaded document.

qa_pairs.question: What is the deadline for submitting proposals for the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: Proposals must be submitted no later than November 4, 2025, at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Standard Time.

qa_pairs.question: Where should Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) proposals be submitted electronically?
qa_pairs.answer: Proposals must be submitted electronically through the CCDB portal at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/resource_allocation_competitions/applications.

qa_pairs.question: Who is responsible for ensuring that a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application is complete and accurate?
qa_pairs.answer: The Principal Investigator (PI) is responsible for ensuring that the application is complete, with all additional documentation uploaded, and that there is no discrepancy between the application document and the online form.

qa_pairs.question: When will applicants be notified about the status of their Resource Allocation Competition applications?
qa_pairs.answer: Applicants will be informed about the status of their applications via email in spring 2026.

qa_pairs.question: Are pre-submission consultations recommended for first-time applicants to the Resource Allocation Competition?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if you are applying for the first time, you are strongly encouraged to consult prior to submitting your application.

qa_pairs.question: By what date should pre-submission consultations occur?
qa_pairs.answer: Consultations should happen prior to October 31, 2025, to allow adequate time for support by technical staff.

qa_pairs.question: What are the goals of a pre-submission consultation?
qa_pairs.answer: The goals are to determine if the resources needed justify a RAC application, verify project eligibility for RPP vs RRG, and provide technical assistance with resource calculation.

qa_pairs.question: How can I schedule a pre-submission consultation?
qa_pairs.answer: To schedule a consultation, you can send an email to allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca or contact your regional support team.

qa_pairs.question: Where are all Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) applications submitted online?
qa_pairs.answer: All RAC applications are submitted online via the CCDB portal at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/resource_allocation_competitions/applications.

qa_pairs.question: What is needed to log in to the CCDB portal for RAC applications?
qa_pairs.answer: Users must log in using an existing Alliance account or register for a new one.

qa_pairs.question: Is an up-to-date Canadian Common CV (CCV) required for the peer review process?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, an up-to-date Canadian Common CV (CCV) is required for the peer review process.

qa_pairs.question: Who is required to upload an up-to-date Canadian Common CV (CCV) with a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: The Principal Investigator (PI) and all Co-PIs are required to upload an up-to-date CCV with any RAC application.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if an updated Canadian Common CV (CCV) is not provided with a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: Failing to provide an updated CCV will negatively impact the overall score of the submitted application.

qa_pairs.question: Can Principal Investigators (PIs) check the status of linked Canadian Common CVs (CCVs) in the online application form?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the PI can view the date in which any CCV linked to the application was last uploaded and whether any action is required in the online application form.

qa_pairs.question: How can Co-PIs update their Canadian Common CVs (CCVs) on CCDB?
qa_pairs.answer: Co-PIs can update their CCVs on CCDB by clicking on 'Update CCV' in the Resource Applications page, or by going to 'My Account → View Reporting' at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/reporting.

qa_pairs.question: Are deadline extensions granted for Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) applications due to missing Canadian Common CVs (CCVs)?
qa_pairs.answer: No, deadline extensions due to missing CCVs are not possible.

qa_pairs.question: What other reporting is required from PIs and Co-PIs related to their use of Federation resources?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs and all Co-PIs are required to report publications enabled by their use of resources provided by the Federation.
